title: Classes, Private State, Inheritance, and Composition · Mastery Check
summary: Build maintainable scheduling components with explicit invariants and replaceable policies. Every item uses a fresh case and misconception-specific distractors. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for a policy-driven scheduling component library. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Explain classes as prototype-based constructs with strict bodies, constructors, methods, and fields.
Use private fields, static fields, and static blocks without confusing instance and constructor state.
Use accessors for stable property semantics without hiding expensive work or surprising side effects.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.javascript:
// javascript-basics cumulative stakeholder program.
// Add one runnable behavior, assertion, changed case, and evidence return per activity.
